worked: **Strawman-then-react spec sessions** — for the consolidation + pinned designs, presenting a concrete (possibly-wrong) architecture + 3-4 concrete options via AskUserQuestion extracted decisions far faster than open-ended questions.

**BUILD.md template discipline** — every design (14, 16, 18) used the atone BUILD.md 9-section shape (Goals/Architecture/FS/Components/Build-order/Acceptance/Failure-modes/Open-questions/Cost/Pointers). Made staged implementation + handoff clean.
**Bisectable per-stage commits** — committing each stage separately meant the skeptical review could pinpoint exactly which change introduced each bug.

**The skeptical-review fork** — caught 4 real bugs an in-context self-review would have rationalized. The standout: I changed shared `parse_json_codeblock` to fix ONE caller (pinned) without checking the other 4 (audit/introspection/metacog/dreaming).
didnt_work: **`cargo install` lag** — every new CLI subcommand (`domain`, `pin`, `audit`) hit "unrecognized subcommand" because the user's shell ran the stale installed binary. `cargo run` works in-tree but the installed `~/.cargo/bin/i-dream` (+ duplicate `~/.local/bin/`) needs `cargo install --path .` after any cli.rs change. Recurred 3×.

**First real dream-pass surfaced 3 parser bugs** synthetic tests missed — real LLM output quotes numbers as strings, omits required fields, and prefixes JSON with prose. Lesson: tolerant deserialization is non-optional for LLM-output parsing.
**Pipe-buffer deadlock** in process-spawning code — draining stdout only after exit deadlocks on >64KB output. Always drain concurrently (reader threads / wait_with_output).

**Test temp-dir races** — `std::process::id()` is constant within a test binary; using it as a "unique" temp dir name + a shared cleanup() causes parallel tests to clobber each other. Use per-call atomic counters.
**The widget icon BTM cache** is genuinely stubborn — a correct bundle in a Spotlight-indexed location + full user-cache purge still showed the generic icon. Unresolved.
notes: The 5 external domains live in the user's home (`~/.claude/{atone,affirm,memory-domain,sessions-domain,pinned}/`), each with `.i-dream-domain.toml` + `dream/prompt.md` — NOT in this repo. The repo only knows them via `DomainRegistry`'s sibling-discovery list (`src/modules/registry.rs`).
`dream-pass` advances a domain's cursor only on success; failed domains retry on next run (cheap — successful domains skip with 0 delta).
The daily digest's richness depends on `dream-pass` having run recently; the briefing depends on the same.
All BUILD docs are at `docs/14`–`docs/19`; roadmap at `docs/15` is the status source of truth.
feedback: Wants per-push approval for `git push origin master` (never blanket — asked every time).
Aggressive over conservative for the L3 audit dials (confidence floor 0.5, max 6/lens).
"Keep going / proceed" = continue autonomously through the queue; only halt at genuine blockers.
Cares about doc/memory/task triple-persistence staying in sync.

pending: **Operational (user-side, optional):** run `i-dream cron install` (daily digest at 03:00); add a weekly `i-dream dream-pass` + the two `extract-events.sh` scripts to a schedule so the briefing stays rich automatically.

**The widget icon** still renders generic in System Settings — 5 untried fixes documented in `docs/rcas/20260521-…` postscript (sudo iconservices cache wipe, bump CFBundleIdentifier, sfltool resetbtm, Developer-ID signing). Cosmetic; widget functions fine.
**V2 refinements (deferred, documented in roadmap + review report):**
L3 audit literal multi-Agent dispatch (v1 ships a single richer prompt structured as 7 voices)
Apply-time `[e]dit-in-$EDITOR` option for audit proposals (v1 is y/c only)
Weekly audit cron plist (waits for user-acceptance of the audit cadence)

**Lower-confidence review observations left intentionally** (per the user's aggressive-tolerance posture): `de_flexible_u32` silently coerces malformed versions to 1; all-default Insight fields swallow typo'd keys; `NoPrompt` status unreachable for external domains; ts→now() fallback non-determinism.
